questions.question: Which describes the discrete line spectrum from gas discharge? (Sec 42.1)
options: a- Continuous rainbow of colors like hot solids
b- Bright lines on dark background
c- Dark lines on bright background only
questions.answer: b- Bright lines on dark background

questions.question: What is the Rydberg formula for Balmer series? 1/λ = ? (Sec 42.1, 42.3, Eq. 42.17)
Why did Rutherford's alpha scattering disprove Thomson's model of the atom? (Sec 42.2)
options: a- All alpha particles passed through with no deflection
b- Some alpha particles scattered backward at large angles
c- Electrons were too heavy to scatter alpha particles
d- No scattering was observed at all
e- The foil absorbed all alpha particles
questions.answer: b- Some alpha particles scattered backward at large angles

questions.question: What classical problem affects Rutherford's planetary model? Use ΔK + ΔU = E_radiated. (Sec 42.2)
options: a- Electron speed becomes zero in orbit
b- Orbiting electron radiates continuously and spirals into nucleus
c- Electron charge becomes zero at high speed
d- Proton radiates instead of electron
e- Electron mass increases without bound
questions.answer: b- Orbiting electron radiates continuously and spirals into nucleus

questions.question: What is Bohr's angular momentum quantization condition for hydrogen? (Sec 42.3, Eq. 42.6)
options: a- m_e v r = n h
b- m_e v r = n ħ, n=1,2,3,…
c- m_e v r = n h/(4π)
questions.answer: b- m_e v r = n ħ, n=1,2,3,…

questions.question: Using E_n = −13.6 eV / n², what is the ground state energy (n=1) of hydrogen? (Sec 42.3, Eq. 42.14)
What is the numerical value of the Bohr radius a_0 for hydrogen? a_0 = ħ² / (m_e k_e e²). (Sec 42.3, Eq. 42.11)
For which transition does the ionization energy of hydrogen (13.6 eV) correspond? (Sec 42.3, Fig. 42.8)
Which set lists the three quantum numbers from solving Schrödinger's equation for hydrogen? (Sec 42.4)
What is the allowed range of orbital quantum number ℓ for a given n? (Sec 42.4)
For n = 2, what are the possible values of ℓ? (Sec 42.4)
Which statement is the Pauli exclusion principle for electrons in an atom? (Sec 42.7)
options: a- Electrons must always pair spins
b- No two electrons can have the same (n, ℓ, m_ℓ, m_s)
c- Only s orbitals can be occupied
questions.answer: b- No two electrons can have the same (n, ℓ, m_ℓ, m_s)

questions.question: Using N_max = 2n², how many electrons can occupy the n=1 shell? (Sec 42.7)
Which spectroscopic label corresponds to ℓ = 1? (Sec 42.4, 42.7)
Why do noble gases have particularly high ionization energies? (Sec 42.7)
options: a- Their outer shells are empty
b- Their outer shells are completely filled and very stable
c- They have very few protons
d- They have extremely large atomic radii
e- They have no electrons in s orbitals
questions.answer: b- Their outer shells are completely filled and very stable

questions.question: In the Balmer series, what is the final principal quantum number n_f in 1/λ = R_H (1/n_f² − 1/n_i²)? (Sec 42.1, 42.3)
Using E_n = −13.6 eV/n², what is E for n=2 in hydrogen? (Sec 42.3, Eq. 42.14)
How many electrons can a p subshell (ℓ = 1) hold in total? (Sec 42.7)
For a 2p orbital, what are the values of n and ℓ? (Sec 42.4)
What is the Balmer series limit wavelength when n_i → ∞ in 1/λ = R_H (1/2² − 1/n_i²)? (Sec 42.1)
